subtitle: Dual-Agent Memory-Native Transformer Architecture
vision: Demonstrate experimentally that memory can exist as a structurally separate functional layer in neural architectures, distinct from both weights (parametric) and context (attentional). The architecture implements a dual-agent design where a writer encoder stores facts and a reader decoder retrieves them through shared semantic address space.
core_goals: Memory-conditioned exact token emission (not just distributional influence)
Distinction between memory addressing competence and memory emission competence
Stable update semantics for same-entity state changes
Compositional memory across multiple semantic clusters
Third category in neural architecture: persistent + addressable + updatable

status.B1_1_extended: PASS on all blocks with compositional limit noted on cross-schema 4-class

what_was_demonstrated: Memory-conditioned exact token emission (94.4% simple, 100% update, 99.2% distractor)
Memory addressing vs emission distinct (97% vs 7% possible in broken versions)
Three compounding architectural bugs: bias pollution, softmax temperature, bank scattering
Update chain stability to length 8 (100% accuracy, zero leak)
Graceful scaling degradation (no cliff, ~1.5pp per added fact beyond 3)

forward_plan.v12: True held-out training (exclude 5 entities completely) - 90min estimated
forward_plan.v14: Gradual crutch removal + bank coherence loss - 180min+
